outlook: UX Researcher positions are experiencing strong growth with 2,667 open roles marking a 41.7% increase from last year, though the $73k median salary remains competitive rather than commanding. The field faces moderate AI disruption risk at 27 out of 100, primarily from synthetic user tools and AI interview summarizers that automate traditional research tasks. However, the rising demand for core human skills like active listening, critical thinking, and reading comprehension suggests the role is evolving toward higher-level strategic work rather than disappearing. While technical skills around data analysis software are becoming less critical as AI handles routine analysis, researchers who can synthesize insights and guide product decisions through nuanced human understanding will remain essential.

skillPivots.headline: Develop strategic research storytelling capabilities
skillPivots.body: Combine your Critical Thinking and Reading Comprehension strengths to create compelling research narratives that influence product strategy. Focus on translating complex user insights into clear business recommendations that drive decision-making at the executive level.

skillPivots.headline: Master facilitation and stakeholder alignment
skillPivots.body: Leverage your Active Listening skills to become the go-to person for cross-functional research workshops and strategy sessions. Build expertise in facilitating design thinking sessions and user journey mapping workshops that bring teams together around user needs.

skillPivots.headline: Specialize in human-AI interaction research
skillPivots.body: Position yourself ahead of Synthetic Users and AI interview tools by becoming an expert in researching how humans interact with AI systems. Focus on studying AI user experience patterns, trust factors, and the evolving relationship between users and intelligent interfaces.
